steps.title: Use the talking clues
steps.content: The phrase appears right after the author says she started to talk on her own. The following details explain the phrase: others disliked riding with her because she was loud and unrelenting, so the issue was nonstop speech.

steps.title: Reject unrelated meanings
steps.content: A treats the phrase as physical restlessness, but the context is talking. B suggests changing her mind, which is not mentioned. C says she was well liked, though the text suggests others found her annoying. The remaining choice captures her constant talking. The answer is D.
